queries.query: cursor continually improving our agent harness keep rate satisfaction 2026
cursorbench real sessions internal eval suite 2026
openai why swe-bench verified no longer measures frontier coding capabilities 2026
anthropic eval awareness browsecomp blocklist benchmark contamination 2026
vercel ai gateway production index july 2026 coding agents routing spend fallbacks

entries.reason: Live A/B testing plus keep-rate and tool-error alerts gives a concrete harness iteration loop with measured operational gains.
Independent guidance reinforces using evals, production monitoring, A/B tests, and transcript review together instead of relying on one layer.
An internal benchmark built from real coding sessions is more aligned than public bug-fix suites and comes with a repeatable maintenance pattern.
Strong evidence that contaminated or flawed public coding benchmarks stop measuring real engineering capability.
OpenAI’s harness-reporting playbook independently supports publishing budgets, harnesses, and validity checks with every serious evaluation claim.
Web-enabled eval integrity needs runtime controls such as blocklists, dataset gating, and trace review, not just careful benchmark design.
Gateway production data shows where cheap models can absorb volume and where frontier models still earn their cost in coding workflows.
The fallback docs provide the concrete routing mechanism behind the workload-based model split.
